title: Open access network secure authentication systems and methods
abstract: Authentication of devices to receive services provided by service providers over communications networks such as open access networks, wherein the provider of the network need not have access to secret information shared between the devices and the service providers. A request is received from a device to receive services from a service provider and is forwarded to the service provider. A challenge and an expected response is received from the service provider and the challenge is forwarded to the device. A response to the challenge is received from the device the device is authenticated to receive services from the service provider if the response received from the device matches the expected response provided by the service provider.
claims:
Claims CLAIMS  What is claimed and desired to be secured by Letters Patent is: 1. A method for authenticating a device to receive services provided by a service provider over a communications network, comprising: receiving a request over a communications network from a network-connected device to receive services from a service provider over the network; forwarding the request to the service provider over the network; receiving a challenge and an expected response from the service provider over the network; forwarding the challenge to the device over the network; receiving a response to the challenge from the device over the network; and authenticating the device to receive services from the service provider if the response received from the device matches the expected response provided by the service provider. 2. The method as recited in claim 1, wherein the service provider differs from a network provider providing infrastructure of the communications network. 3. The method as recited in claim 2, wherein the network provider authenticates the device to receive services from the service provider without knowing a shared secret shared between the service provider and the device. 4. The method as recited in claim 2, further comprising notifying the service provider that authentication was successful and establishing a network connection between the service provider and the device. 5. The method as recited in claim 2, wherein the network connection between the service provider and the device is a service logical network. 6. The method as recited in claim 1, further comprising establishing a bootstrapping logical network between the device and an authentication portal to be used during the authentication process. 7. The method as recited in claim 6, wherein network and service provider resources accessible over the bootstrapping logical network are restricted to resources necessary to provide authentication. 8. A method for authenticating a device to receive services provided by a service provider over an open-access communications network, comprising: 

 receiving a request over an open-access communications network provided by a network provider from a network-connected device to receive services from a service provider over the network; using network resources to forward the request to the service provider over the network; receiving a challenge and an expected response from the service provider over the network; forwarding the challenge to the device over the network; receiving a response to the challenge from the device over the network; and authenticating the device to receive services from the service provider if the response received from the device matches the expected response provided by the service provider. 9. The method as recited in claim 8, wherein the service provider and the network provider are different entities. 10. The method as recited in claim 9, wherein the network provider authenticates the device to receive services from the service provider without knowing a shared secret shared between the service provider and the device. 11. The method as recited in claim 9, further comprising notifying the service provider that authentication was successful and establishing a network connection between the service provider and the device. 12. The method as recited in claim 9, wherein the network connection between the service provider and the device is a service logical network. 13. The method as recited in claim 8, further comprising establishing a bootstrapping logical network between the device and an authentication portal to be used during the authentication process. 14. The method as recited in claim 13, wherein network and service provider resources available over the bootstrapping logical network are restricted to resources necessary to provide authentication. 15. A system for providing authentication services between an access device communicatively connected to an open access network and a service provider with infrastructure communicatively connected to the open access network comprising: an open access network communicatively connected to an access device and infrastructure provided by a service provider; and a network operator authentication server configured to: 

 receive a request over the network from the access device to receive services from the service provider over the network; forward the request to the service provider over the network; receive a challenge and an expected response from the service provider over the network; forward the challenge to the access device over the network; receive a response to the challenge from the device over the network; and authenticate the device to receive services from the service provider if the response received from the device matches the expected response provided by the service provider. 16. The system as recited in claim 15, wherein the network operator authentication server is further configured to provide new devices subscribing to the network with a virtual subscriber ID manager and a network operator profile to be managed by the virtual subscriber ID manager. 17. The system as recited in claim 15, wherein the system further comprises a bootstrapping logical network established on infrastructure of the open access network to establish a communicative connection between the access device and the network authentication server. 18. The system as recited in claim 15, wherein the system further comprises a portal logical network established on infrastructure of the open access network to establish a communicative connection between the network authentication server and infrastructure of the service provider. 19. The system as recited in claim 18, wherein the portal logical network communicatively connects with a service provider authentication server. 20. The system of claim 15, wherein after authentication is complete, the system further comprises a service logical network established on infrastructure of the open access network to establish a communicative connection between the access device and infrastructure of the service provider.
